mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware accelerators and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the llm-d deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count, which is required to run llm-d.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d Helm chart into the Kubernetes cluster, deploying the necessary resources.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> Installs the llm-d application in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstalls the llm-d application.
> Deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This step involves upgrading the llm-d installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backs up current configurations.
> Upgrades llm-d to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and verify your configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated to meet the demands of llm-d.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get services -n llm-d'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators, improved autoscaling features, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
